evals.expected_output: Should flag: no versioning, no auth model, no error format, no pagination for GET /orders, no idempotency keys for POST, sequential IDs likely, no health checks, no rate limiting. Should ask about: consumers (public or internal?), scale, deployment. Should NOT generate code.

evals.prompt:
Please review this OpenAPI spec:

openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Payments API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /v1/payments:
    post:
      summary: Create payment
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [amount, currency]
              properties:
                amount:
                  type: integer
                currency:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Payment created
  /v1/payments/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get payment
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Payment details
evals.expected_output: Should extract context from spec (payments domain, versioned). Should flag: sequential integer IDs (type: integer for payment ID is an enumeration risk for a payment API), no auth defined in spec, no error responses defined, no idempotency key for POST /payments (critical for payments), no Idempotency-Key header, no rate limiting, amount as integer without specifying smallest currency unit, no pagination for list endpoint. Should recognize good: versioning present (/v1/), contract-first approach. Should NOT ask redundant context questions already answered by the spec.

evals.prompt: I'm building an API for a mobile fitness app. Users can track workouts, follow other users, and view a social feed. We expect about 10k DAU growing to 100k. What should I think about?
evals.expected_output: Should ask clarifying questions first (not jump to review): auth approach, team experience, deployment target, real-time needs for social feed. Should suggest considering: SSE or WebSockets for the social feed (not just REST polling), cursor-based pagination for feed endpoints, privacy controls for follow relationships (BOLA risk), rate limiting on social actions (prevent spam follows), caching strategy for feed. Should flag that 10k-100k DAU implies moderate scale requiring attention to resilience but not extreme measures. Should NOT produce a full review document yet — needs more context first.
